description: Set the tolerance used when updating node-to-zone links in response to large-strain motion. A node is considered to be in a zone if it is within a distance \(\delta\) of the zone boundary, where \(\delta\) equals f times zone size (taken as the maximum \(x\) -, \(y\) -, or \(z\) -dimension of the zone bounding box). By default, f equals 1 × 10 -5 .

6.0.description: Set the tolerance used when updating node-to-zone links in response to large-strain motion. A node is considered to be in a zone if it is within a distance d of the zone boundary, where δ equals f times zone size (taken as the maximum x-, y-, or z-dimension of the zone bounding box). By default, f equals 1 × 10-5.

7.0.description: Set the tolerance used when updating node-to-zone links in response to large-strain motion. A node is considered to be in a zone if it is within a distance:math`delta` of the zone boundary, where:math`delta` equals f times zone size (taken as the maximum \(x\)-, \(y\)-, or \(z\)-dimension of the zone bounding box). By default, f equals 1 × 10-5.
